meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Learn how to use project management, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build a profitable MVP as a beginner founder.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you shift from passive learning to active project management. By breaking your vision into modular, trackable tasks and utilizing AI-driven coding agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform abstract ideas into shippable features, ensuring every line of code serves your business objective.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'Build a To-Do App' video, but your own vision remains a ghost in the machine. You are trapped in the purgatory of endless tutorials—consuming content while your product remains unbuilt, unlaunched, and unmonetized. This isn't a lack of talent; it is a failure of systemization.

To move from spectator to founder, you must treat your build like a professional engineering project. By applying rigorous project management to your development lifecycle, you cut through the noise and force your AI tools to work for your business, not just your education.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Strip your idea down to the single core feature that provides value. Do not build a platform; build a utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Analyze my idea for [Product Name] and define the smallest possible feature set for a 7-day MVP launch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project roadmap file named MVP_ROADMAP.md based on the provided core features.'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, define how your data flows. A solid schema prevents technical debt later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for my MVP. Prioritize scalability and simplicity. Output in SQL format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the Prisma schema based on the database requirements defined in MVP_ROADMAP.md.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Use boilerplate and AI to set up your environment so you can focus on business logic immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, production-ready project structure using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project directory and install all necessary dependencies listed in the prompt.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Implement features one by one using the roadmap. Never branch out until the current feature is fully tested.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the authentication flow for my MVP. Ensure it is secure and handles edge cases like password resets.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the authentication module and integrate it with the Supabase client.'

steps.title: Validation and Deployment
steps.description: Connect your MVP to a live environment early to collect feedback from actual users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist for production deployment, including environment variables, security headers, and domain setup.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify all build steps and generate a deployment summary for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Never ask an AI to 'build an app.' Ask for 'a modular implementation of X feature' to maintain control.
If your code output exceeds 200 lines, you are trying to do too much at once. Break it into smaller, manageable files.
Treat your project management file like a contract; if a task isn't in the roadmap, don't build it.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for every function before you start building the UI around it.
Shipping a buggy version is infinitely better than having a perfect version that never leaves your local machine.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus exclusively on user feedback loops. Do not add features based on your own assumptions; add them based on the friction points your first 10 users report.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the workflow of a high-velocity founder. Stop watching and start building the future you envisioned. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
